question: What cooling method is used for the compute trays in the GB200 NVL72, and what cooling method handles the power shelves?
findings: The GB200 NVL72 is described as a fully liquid-cooled rack-scale design that interconnects all nodes with NVIDIA NVLink technology.
Each GB200 NVL72 compute tray (GIGABYTE XN14-CB0-LA01) is a 1U liquid-cooled server node whose cooling specification includes 8 x 40x40x56mm fans and 2 x Superchip cold plate loops.
The GB200 NVL72 compute trays use liquid cooling via two superchip cold plate loops, and each tray also includes 8 fans under its cooling specification.
The GB200 NVL72 rack includes three 1U 33kW power shelves that are compatible with an in-rack CDU or in-row CDU for liquid cooling.

The GB200 NVL72 rack includes 2 x OOB management switches, 1 x optional OS switch, 3 x 1U 33kW power shelves, and 10 x compute trays, with power shelf cooling compatible with in-rack CDU or in-row CDU.

research_gaps.description: No power delivery or PDU infrastructure guidance found.
No total heat rejection load specification found.
No UPS or battery backup requirements found.
No coolant supply temperature or flow rate specifications found.
